blocks.text: Fernhollow Partners LLC
Re: Engagement for Post-Close Integration Support
Dear Amanda Golden:

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}} has engaged Fernhollow Partners LLC to provide post-close integration support. Work begins {{fact:f:E-2024-001.start}} and is expected to run approximately eight months. This letter states the scope, the approach, the team, and the fee. If any term does not match your understanding, call me before you sign.

The scope is integration of the acquired operations into your finance function. We will map the chart of accounts, align the monthly close and reporting calendar, reconcile opening balances, and prepare the working papers your auditors will expect. Anything outside this scope will be raised with you in writing before we do the work.

Our approach is the one we use on every post-close engagement. We establish the facts first, put the figures in front of you early, and keep the written record short enough to be read. You will not receive a deliverable we have not tied out.

Sandra Perez, Director, will run the engagement day to day and is your first call. Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, will prepare the working drafts and readouts. Ryan Strong, Analyst, will build and tie out the supporting schedules. I will read every deliverable before it reaches you.
The fee is {{fact:f:E-2024-001.fee}}, fixed for the scope described above and invoiced monthly. Reasonable out-of-pocket expenses are billed at cost. There are no other charges.
We value the confidence Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez has placed in the firm. Please sign where indicated below and return one copy to my attention. Work begins on the start date stated above.
Meeting Minutes: Post-Close Integration Support for {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}}
Working session on the integration workstreams. Minutes prepared by Ryan Strong, Analyst, and circulated to all attendees for correction.
Attendees

items: Briana Dennis, Senior Associate, Fernhollow Partners
Amanda Golden, VP Finance, Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez

blocks.text: Discussion

Sandra Perez opened with the state of the chart of accounts mapping, which is substantially complete. The remaining open items sit in the intercompany balances, where several entries could not yet be traced to source. I have flagged each of these rather than carrying them forward unsupported, and Sandra Perez agreed that the flags should stand until the client can confirm the underlying records.

Briana Dennis walked through the draft reporting calendar and the points where it still diverges from the client's existing close routine. Amanda Golden confirmed that her team can provide the supporting detail for the flagged intercompany items and asked that requests continue to come in batches rather than piecemeal, which suits our current practice.
Action Items

rows: Circulate the revised chart of accounts mapping for client review
Tie out the flagged intercompany balances against client records
Provide source detail for the flagged items
Set the agenda and confirm attendance for the next working session

blocks.text: Status Report: Post-Close Integration Support for {{fact:f:E-2024-001.client}}
Prepared by Sandra Perez, Director, Fernhollow Partners, for the executive team of Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez.
Status Summary

The engagement is on track. What changed this period: the chart of accounts mapping was adopted, and the first combined monthly close ran under the aligned calendar. What it means: your reporting now comes off one structure, and the remaining work is documentation and cleanup rather than design. The one open thread is a small set of intercompany balances still awaiting source detail from your team.
Accomplished This Period

items: Chart of accounts mapping adopted and in use for the combined close
First combined monthly close completed under the aligned reporting calendar
Opening balance reconciliations completed, except the flagged intercompany items Ryan Strong is holding open pending client records

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

items: Audit-ready working papers for the opening balances
Close process documentation for handoff to your controller group
First draft of the board readout, prepared by Briana Dennis

blocks.text: Risks and Requests

Year-end audit requests will compete for your staff's time this period, and the flagged intercompany items cannot close without their input. What we need from you: the source detail already requested, and a named reviewer for the close documentation, both before the next working session. Neither request is large; both are on the critical path.
Budget
Work performed to date remains within the fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2024-001.fee}} agreed in the engagement letter. No change to scope or budget is requested.
